evals.prompt: How do I create a standalone Angular component?
evals.expected_output: Add standalone: true (or omit in Angular 20+) to @Component. Import all dependencies directly in the component's imports array. Do not declare in NgModule. Use ng generate c to scaffold automatically.

evals.prompt: How do I use @if and @for in Angular templates?
evals.expected_output: Use @if (condition) { } and @for (item of items; track item.id) { } instead of *ngIf/*ngFor. Use @empty { } block inside @for for empty state. Use @switch for multi-branch conditionals.

evals.prompt: How do I use signal inputs with input.required() in Angular?
evals.expected_output: Replace @Input() with input<T>() or input.required<T>(). Use booleanAttribute transform for boolean inputs. Access value as a function: this.disabled().

evals.prompt: How do I bind host properties in Angular without @HostBinding?
evals.expected_output: Define host bindings in the host: {} object on @Component: host: { '[class.active]': 'isActive()', '(click)': 'onClick()' }. Never use @HostBinding or @HostListener decorators.

evals.prompt: How do I use output() instead of EventEmitter in Angular?
evals.expected_output: Use output<T>() from @angular/core (v17.3+) instead of @Output() EventEmitter<T>. Emit with: this.selected.emit(value). For two-way binding use model() which creates a writable signal.

evals.prompt: How do I properly set up OnPush change detection in Angular?
evals.expected_output: Add changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush to @Component. Component only re-renders when Signal inputs change or Signals fire. Never use default change detection.

evals.prompt: What is the Smart/Dumb component pattern in Angular?
evals.expected_output: Smart (Container) components inject services and manage state. Dumb (Presentational) components receive data via inputs and emit events via outputs only, with no service dependencies.

assertions.value: inputs and emit events via outputs

evals.prompt: Should I use computed signals or functions in Angular templates?
evals.expected_output: Never call functions in templates: {{ calculate() }} re-evaluates on every change detection cycle. Use computed() for derived values — it caches the result.

evals.prompt: How do I avoid memory leaks when subscribing in Angular components?
evals.expected_output: Use toSignal() which auto-unsubscribes, or takeUntilDestroyed() for manual subscriptions. Inject DestroyRef for cleanup. Never call subscribe() in ngOnInit without a cleanup strategy.

should_not_trigger: How do I create a React functional component with hooks?
